amendments.normalized_body: In `engine.rs:4671-4672`, after restoring `snapshot.active_run` from `interrupted_run.take()` and setting `status = RunStatus::Running`, the snapshot is NOT persisted to disk. If the process crashes between this in-memory restoration and the next `write_run_snapshot` call (at the top of the next loop iteration), the on-disk state still has `status=Failed, active_run=None, interrupted_run=Some(...)`. On resume, `run resume` picks up `interrupted_run` correctly so recovery works, but consider adding a `write_run_snapshot` after line 4672 to make the on-disk state match the in-memory state. This would prevent a scenario where an external observer (status polling, UI) sees a stale 'Failed' status during the brief window between restoration and the next stage dispatch.

The `classify_exit_failure` function in `process_backend.rs:1496-1501` only special-cases exit code 127 (BinaryNotFound). All other non-zero exits AND signal-killed processes (where `status.code()` returns `None`) are classified as `TransportFailure` (retryable, 5 attempts). While the spec explicitly says signal kills should be retryable, consider logging a warning when a process is killed by a signal (SIGSEGV=11, SIGABRT=6) since repeated signal kills strongly suggest a deterministic crash rather than a transient issue. A warning would help operators notice this pattern without changing retry behavior.

Treat failure to persist the pre-backoff snapshot as fatal in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4622-4644` instead of ignoring `write_run_snapshot()`. The new crash-resume path only works if that `Failed`/`interrupted_run` snapshot actually reaches disk; today the write error is silently swallowed, so a kill or crash during backoff can still leave the project stranded in the old `Running` snapshot with no resumable state.

Preserve retry-attempt state across a crash during backoff. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4622-4644` now writes resumable mid-retry snapshots, but `derive_resume_state()` rebuilds every failed resume with `attempt: 1` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5851-5857`. A restart during the backoff window therefore replenishes the retry budget and can exceed the configured max-attempt limit for the stage.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: The `classify_exit_failure` function in `process_backend.rs:1496-1501` only special-cases exit code 127 (BinaryNotFound). All other non-zero exits AND signal-killed processes (where `status.code()` returns `None`) are classified as `TransportFailure` (retryable, 5 attempts). While the spec explicitly says signal kills should be retryable, consider logging a warning when a process is killed by a signal (SIGSEGV=11, SIGABRT=6) since repeated signal kills strongly suggest a deterministic crash rather than a transient issue. A warning would help operators notice this pattern without changing retry behavior.

Treat failure to persist the pre-backoff snapshot as fatal in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4622-4644` instead of ignoring `write_run_snapshot()`. The new crash-resume path only works if that `Failed`/`interrupted_run` snapshot actually reaches disk; today the write error is silently swallowed, so a kill or crash during backoff can still leave the project stranded in the old `Running` snapshot with no resumable state.

Preserve retry-attempt state across a crash during backoff. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4622-4644` now writes resumable mid-retry snapshots, but `derive_resume_state()` rebuilds every failed resume with `attempt: 1` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5851-5857`. A restart during the backoff window therefore replenishes the retry budget and can exceed the configured max-attempt limit for the stage.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 3 amendment(s); restart required.
